OCR Evaluation — medicine_package

[OCR] Fix 90°/270° orientation ambiguity via OCR confidence tiebreaker; fix annotation alignment 6e55748d64a7403a63b0ee516a7a8083d56cd02d Projection-profile variance is symmetric under 180° row-reversal, so 90° and 270° always score identically. Added _resolve_rotation_ambiguity() in ocr_engine.py: runs a quick, downsampled OCR pass without angle correction on both candidates — right-side-up text scores much higher confidence than inverted text. IMG_4256 medicine tube CER drops from 83.7% to 45.9%. Also fixed _annotate_image_b64 in eval_fixtures.py to apply correct_orientation before drawing bboxes, so the HTML report shows the same orientation the OCR pipeline actually processed. Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com> eval_20260628_001  ·  2026-06-28 00:53:47.127173 UTC
NEEDS IMPROVEMENT 44.8% mean CER  (threshold < 30%)

Mean CER 44.8% exceeds the 30% threshold. Failed: IMG_4256.JPG (45.9%), IMG_4257.JPG (84.2%). Passed: IMG_4255.JPG (4.3%). Failure mode(s): high CER despite tokens detected — likely reading-order errors on curved/rotated surfaces (IMG_4257.JPG); partial recognition — tokens missing or substituted (IMG_4256.JPG).

Character Error Rate (CER) — edit distance between recognised and expected text, divided by the length of the expected text. Lower is better; 0% = perfect match.
< 10% excellent 10–30% acceptable 30–60% poor > 60% failing > 100% hypothesis longer than reference
IMG_4255.JPG
1.59s ✓ CER 4.3%
